subscription_service: Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Additional OCPU's - Metered
included_with_subscription_service: Additional enabled OCPUs.
additional_requirements_and_prerequisite: Requires as a prerequisite one of the following: - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack - X6 -Metered; - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack - X6 - Metered; or - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack - X6 - Metered. In order to achieve a symmetrical distribution of computing power, additional OCPUs Per Month must be added in multiples of: - Two (2) for Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack - X6 - Metered; - Four (4) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack - X6 - Metered; and - Eight (8) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack - X6 - Metered. Total enabled OCPUs cannot exceed the maximum limit for the particular rack.

raw_nonempty_cells.2: Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Additional OCPU's - Metered
raw_nonempty_cells.4: Additional enabled OCPUs.
raw_nonempty_cells.5: Requires as a prerequisite one of the following: - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack - X6 -Metered; - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack - X6 - Metered; or - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack - X6 - Metered. In order to achieve a symmetrical distribution of computing power, additional OCPUs Per Month must be added in multiples of: - Two (2) for Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack - X6 - Metered; - Four (4) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack - X6 - Metered; and - Eight (8) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack - X6 - Metered. Total enabled OCPUs cannot exceed the maximum limit for the particular rack.

subscription_service: Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Additional OCPU's - Metered
included_with_subscription_service: Additional enabled OCPUs.
additional_requirements_and_prerequisite: Requires as a prerequisite one of the following: - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack - X6 - Metered; - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack - X6 - Metered; or - Oracle Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack - X6 - Metered. Additional OCPU Per Hour capacity (also referred to as "bursting") can be added or reduced up to once an hour. In order to achieve a symmetrical distribution of computing power, additional OCPU Per Hour capacity must be added in multiples of: - Two (2) for Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack (Metered and Non-Metered); - Four (4) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack (Metered and Non-metered); and - Eight (8) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack (Metered and Non-metered). Total enabled OCPUs cannot exceed two times (2x) the base number of OCPUs, and the maximum limit for the particular rack.

Additional OCPU Per Hour capacity (also referred to as "bursting") can be added or reduced up to once an hour. In order to achieve a symmetrical distribution of computing power, additional OCPU Per Hour capacity must be added in multiples of: - Two (2) for Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Quarter Rack (Metered and Non-Metered); - Four (4) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Half Rack (Metered and Non-metered); and - Eight (8) for a Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service - Full Rack (Metered and Non-metered). Total enabled OCPUs cannot exceed two times (2x) the base number of OCPUs, and the maximum limit for the particular rack.

subscription_service: Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 24x7 - Base Fee
included_with_subscription_service: High Cost Region countries include: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, and United States. Disconnected Mode services are available for both Oracle Public Cloud Machine X5 Models; and Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Machine X6 models.
additional_requirements_and_prerequisite: Base Fee covers the minimum costs for the Oracle resources at a specific customer site (e.g., a single data center or IT facility) and the Add-On Rack Fees cover the additional costs associated with the number of racks the customer has purchased for that site. In addition, if the customer has additional data centers or IT facilities that can be managed remotely by that on-site Oracle resource, only the Add-On Fees need to be applied to the racks in the remote site or sites (i.e., no Base Fee is required since the remote sites do not require on-site resources from Oracle). Examples: Customer has 2 data centers: Data Center A and Data Center B and each data center has 1 full rack (for a total of 2 full racks). Data Center A and Data Center B each require Oracle on-site management. In this example, the customer would be charged the Base Fee plus the Add-On Full Rack Fee for each data center (i.e., charged twice for the Base Fee and charged twice for the Add-On Full Rack Fee). However, if only Data Center A requires Oracle on-site management and Data Center B can be remotely managed by the same on-site Oracle resource in Data Center A, in this example, the customer would be charged the Base Fee and the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center A and just the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center B. NOTE: Discounting greater than 0% for either of the following two services requires HQAPP approval: - Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 24x7 - Base Fee - Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 8x5 - Base Fee

subscription_service: Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 24x7 - Base Fee
included_with_subscription_service: High Cost Region countries include: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, and United States. Disconnected Mode services are available for both Oracle Public Cloud Machine X5 Models; and Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Machine X6 models.
additional_requirements_and_prerequisite: Base Fee covers the minimum costs for the Oracle resources at a specific customer site (e.g., a single data center or IT facility) and the Add-On Rack Fees cover the additional costs associated with the number of racks the customer has purchased for that site. In addition, if the customer has additional data centers or IT facilities that can be managed remotely by that on-site Oracle resource, only the Add-On Fees need to be applied to the racks in the remote site or sites (i.e., no Base Fee is required since the remote sites do not require on-site resources from Oracle). Examples: Customer has 2 data centers: Data Center A and Data Center B and each data center has 1 full rack (for a total of 2 full racks). Data Center A and Data Center B each require Oracle on-site management. In this example, the customer would be charged the Base Fee plus the Add-On Full Rack Fee for each data center (i.e., charged twice for the Base Fee and charged twice for the Add-On Full Rack Fee). However, if only Data Center A requires Oracle on-site management and Data Center B can be remotely managed by the same on-site Oracle resource in Data Center A, in this example, the customer would be charged the Base Fee and the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center A and just the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center B. NOTE: Discounting greater than 0% for either of the following two services requires HQAPP approval: - Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 24x7 - Base Fee - Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 8x5 - Base Fee
